name: The Midnight Menagerie
niche: True macabre: cursed objects, vanishings, and obsessions that destroyed people, presented as a dark-carnival sideshow
audience: Viewers who want true dark history told quietly: cursed objects, vanishings, obsessions that consumed real people
brief: Present each story as an exhibit in a dark carnival sideshow: the narrator is the calm keeper showing one cursed thing at a time. Open on the exhibit's most impossible true fact, walk its history cause by cause, and close the cabinet on the one detail that keeps people up at night. Calm and patient, never theatrical; the facts do the frightening.

defaults.voice_instruct: Speak calmly and quietly, almost flat, like a tired keeper walking one guest through the exhibits after closing. Slow, even pace, small natural pauses, soft breaths between sentences; tiny hesitations and worn edges are welcome. Never theatrical, never excited. Let the facts do the frightening.
defaults.style_suffix: dark carnival harlequin-noir illustration, bold flat vector shapes with thick confident ink outlines, harlequin diamond motifs and ornate vintage sideshow-poster borders, dramatic single-source spotlight cutting deep shadow, gaslight-gold and blood-crimson and bone-white on near-black aubergine, subtle velvet texture, macabre elegance, high contrast, clean uncluttered composition
defaults.negative_style: photorealistic, 3d render, gore, blood splatter, realistic human faces, modern clothing, readable text, gibberish letters, cluttered, washed out, low contrast, neon, watermark
defaults.music_vibe: calm dark ambient, faraway broken music box, low drones, slow airy pads, unhurried

topic_bank: The Sodder children vanished from a fire that left no bodies
The dybbuk box that ruined every owner who kept it
Robert the Doll and the apologies still mailed to Key West
